LHX.sentence: L3Harris ticked up a little, and even though its profit margins are running thinner than the bigger defense companies right now, steady military spending keeps the underlying business on solid footing.

ASML.sentence: ASML slipped a touch this week, which is nothing to worry about for the only company on earth that makes the machines needed to print the most advanced computer chips.

LIN.sentence: Linde is having an utterly quiet week — they supply oxygen and other industrial gases under long contracts and nudge prices up steadily, which is the whole story.

MSFT.sentence: Microsoft jumped a bit and is actually looking unusually cheap after pulling back from its high, even as its cloud and AI business keeps grinding forward.

TMO.sentence: Thermo Fisher is steady, and the lab-equipment slump that's held it back appears to be bottoming out, which is the part worth watching.

ENB.sentence: Enbridge is flat and looking a touch expensive lately — its pipelines run like steady toll roads, but investors have bid it up to the point where there's not much bargain left.

NOW.sentence: No fresh data on ServiceNow today, so there's nothing new to report on the workflow-automation software company.

GLNCY.sentence: Glencore edged up, and while its mining profits are at an unusually low ebb right now, that kind of trough is often the better time to own a commodity company.

IBIT.sentence: Bitcoin is down near its low for the year after a rough stretch, so it's been beaten up, though it gained a little today.

KOF.sentence: The Latin American Coca-Cola bottler is quiet, helped by a stable Mexican peso, which is the main thing that moves this one for U.S. investors.

PBR.A.sentence: Petrobras dipped a bit today but is still paying out a very generous dividend, though that depends on oil staying high and on Brazil's government not redirecting the cash elsewhere.

AMKBY.sentence: Maersk, the container-shipping giant, rose a bit and is trading below the value of its own fleet of ships — a sign it's cheaply priced right now.
